questions.question: Q51. I would rather stay at home _______ go out in the heavy rain.
questions.explanation: "Rather" is always followed by the conjunction "than" to show preference. "Rather" के साथ हमेशा "than" का प्रयोग तुलना या पसंद बताने के लिए किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q52. He had no other option _______ to apologize for his behavior.
questions.explanation: After "no other", the conjunction "than" must be used. "No other" के बाद हमेशा "than" का प्रयोग व्याकरण के अनुसार सही होता है।

questions.question: Q53. No sooner had the bell rung _______ the students ran out of the classroom.
questions.explanation: "No sooner" is a correlative conjunction that always takes "than". "No sooner" के साथ हमेशा "than" का जोड़ा (pair) बनता है।

questions.question: Q54. Hardly had I reached the station _______ the train started moving.
questions.explanation: "Hardly" and "Scarcely" are followed by "when" or "before". "Hardly" और "Scarcely" के साथ "when" या "before" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q55. Scarcely had she finished her speech _______ the audience started cheering.
questions.explanation: "Scarcely" requires "when" to complete the sentence structure. "Scarcely" के साथ "when" का प्रयोग किया जाता है, "than" का नहीं।

questions.question: Q56. No sooner did the thief see the police _______ he took to his heels.
questions.explanation: Even with "did", "no sooner" maintains its pair with "than". "No sooner" के साथ "did" आए या "had", हमेशा "than" ही लगेगा।

questions.question: Q57. Scarcely had I entered the room _______ the light went out.
questions.explanation: "Scarcely" is followed by "when" to indicate an immediate sequence of events. "Scarcely" के साथ "when" का प्रयोग तुरंत होने वाली क्रिया के लिए होता है।

questions.question: Q58. She is _______ taller of the two sisters.
questions.explanation: When comparing two people/things using "of the two", use "the + comparative". दो के बीच तुलना में "of the two" होने पर comparative degree से पहले "the" लगता है।

questions.question: Q59. Of the two paths, this one is _______ shorter.
questions.explanation: Use the definite article "the" before a comparative degree when "of the two" is present. "Of the two" के साथ comparative degree से पहले "the" का प्रयोग अनिवार्य है।

questions.question: Q60. He is the _______ of the two candidates interviewed today.
questions.explanation: For two persons, the comparative degree "better" is used with "the". दो व्यक्तियों के लिए "the" के साथ comparative degree (better) का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q61. Although he worked very hard, _______ he failed the examination.
questions.explanation: "Although" or "Though" is followed by "yet" or a comma. "Although" के साथ "yet" का प्रयोग होता है या सिर्फ comma लगाया जाता है।

questions.question: Q62. Though she was ill, _______ she attended the office.
questions.explanation: "Though" pairs with "yet" to show contrast in a sentence. "Though" के साथ "yet" का प्रयोग विपरीत स्थिति (contrast) दिखाने के लिए होता है।

questions.question: Q63. Run fast lest you _______ miss the train.
questions.explanation: "Lest" is always followed by the modal verb "should". "Lest" के साथ हमेशा "should" का प्रयोग किया जाता है, जिसका अर्थ 'कहीं ऐसा न हो' होता है।

questions.question: Q64. Work hard lest you _______ fail in the final test.
questions.explanation: "Lest" requires "should" regardless of the tense of the main clause. "Lest" के साथ हमेशा "should" का ही प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q65. Both Ravi _______ his friend have decided to join the army.
questions.explanation: "Both" is always paired with "and", never "as well as". "Both" का सही जोड़ा "and" है, "as well as" का प्रयोग गलत है।

questions.question: Q66. Both the minister and the secretary _______ invited to the function.
questions.explanation: "Both...and" joins two subjects and takes a plural verb. "Both...and" से जुड़े दो subjects के लिए plural verb "were" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q67. Not only the students but also the teacher _______ present in the lab.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the nearest subject "teacher" (singular). "Not only...but also" में verb पास वाले subject "teacher" के अनुसार singular होगा।

questions.question: Q68. Not only he but also his parents _______ to attend the wedding.
questions.explanation: The verb "wants" follows the plural subject "parents" (Correction: want). (Re-ex: Verb follows plural 'parents' so 'want' is correct).

questions.question: Q69. Neither the principal nor the lecturers _______ attending the seminar.
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with the nearest subject "lecturers" which is plural. Verb सबसे पास वाले subject "lecturers" (plural) के अनुसार "are" होगा।

questions.question: Q70. Either the students or their guide _______ responsible for the project.
questions.explanation: "Either...or" takes the verb according to the nearest subject "guide". "Either...or" में verb पास वाले subject "guide" (singular) के अनुसार "is" होगा।

questions.question: Q71. I have never seen _______ a beautiful flower before.
questions.explanation: Use "such" before "a/an + adjective + noun". "A/an + adjective + noun" से पहले "such" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q72. The movie was _______ interesting that I watched it twice.
questions.explanation: "So" is followed by an adjective and then "that". Adjective के बाद "that" होने पर "so" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q73. It was _______ a cold day that we stayed indoors.
questions.explanation: "Such" is used with a noun phrase (a cold day) followed by "that". Noun phrase के साथ "such...that" का प्रयोग सही है।

questions.question: Q74. He is _______ weak that he cannot walk properly.
questions.explanation: "So...that" is used to show cause and effect. "So...that" का प्रयोग कारण और परिणाम बताने के लिए किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q75. Unless you _______ hard, you will not succeed.
questions.explanation: "Unless" itself is negative; do not use "not" in its clause. "Unless" अपने आप में negative है, इसलिए इसके साथ "not" का प्रयोग नहीं होता।

questions.question: Q76. Until the rain _______, we will stay under the shed.
questions.explanation: "Until" refers to time and takes the simple present tense. "Until" समय बताता है और इसके साथ simple present tense का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q77. You cannot enter the hall _______ you have a valid ticket.
questions.explanation: "Unless" is used for a condition, whereas "until" is for time. "Unless" शर्त (condition) के लिए और "until" समय के लिए उपयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q78. Wait here _______ I come back from the market.
questions.explanation: "Until" is used here to indicate the time limit. समय की सीमा बताने के लिए यहाँ "until" का प्रयोग सही है।

questions.question: Q79. I would prefer to die _______ beg for money.
questions.explanation: "Prefer" generally takes "to", but with "would prefer" + infinitive, use "rather than". "Would prefer" के साथ तुलना में "rather than" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q80. She prefers tea _______ coffee in the morning.
questions.explanation: The verb "prefer" is followed by the preposition "to". "Prefer" के साथ हमेशा preposition "to" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q81. This book is preferable _______ that one for beginners.
questions.explanation: "Preferable" is always followed by "to", not "than". "Preferable" के साथ हमेशा "to" लगता है, "than" का प्रयोग गलत है।

questions.question: Q82. She is the _______ of all the students in the class.
questions.explanation: For more than two people, use the superlative degree "tallest". दो से अधिक लोगों के लिए superlative degree "tallest" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q83. This is the _______ interesting book I have ever read.
questions.explanation: Use "most" to form the superlative degree for long adjectives. लंबे adjectives के लिए superlative degree बनाने हेतु "most" का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Q84. He is senior _______ me in terms of experience.
questions.explanation: Adjectives ending in "-ior" take "to" instead of "than". "-ior" पर खत्म होने वाले adjectives (senior, junior) के साथ "to" लगता है।

questions.question: Q85. My brother is junior _______ all the other employees.
questions.explanation: "Junior" always takes "to" for comparison. "Junior" के साथ तुलना के लिए हमेशा "to" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q86. This task is prior _______ any other work I have today.
questions.explanation: "Prior" is a Latin adjective that takes the preposition "to". "Prior" के साथ हमेशा "to" preposition का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q87. The climate of Pune is better than _______ of Mumbai.
questions.explanation: Use "that of" to compare singular uncountable nouns like climate. Singular noun (climate) की तुलना के लिए "that of" का प्रयोग करते हैं।

questions.question: Q88. The streets of Delhi are wider than _______ of Mumbai.
questions.explanation: Use "those of" to compare plural nouns like streets. Plural noun (streets) की तुलना के लिए "those of" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q89. He has _______ money than his friend.
questions.explanation: "Less" is the comparative of "little" used for uncountable money. Uncountable (money) के लिए "little" का comparative "less" प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q90. There are _______ students in the class today than yesterday.
questions.explanation: "Fewer" is the comparative of "few" used for countable nouns. Countable nouns (students) के लिए "fewer" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q91. He is the _______ student in the entire school.
questions.explanation: Use the superlative degree "most intelligent" for a group. पूरे समूह में तुलना के लिए superlative degree "most intelligent" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q92. This is a _______ better solution to the problem.
questions.explanation: "Much" can modify a comparative degree adjective. "Much" का प्रयोग comparative degree को और प्रभावशाली बनाने के लिए किया जा सकता है।

questions.question: Q93. The higher you go, _______ cooler it becomes.
questions.explanation: In parallel comparison, use "the" with both comparatives. समानांतर तुलना में दोनों comparative degrees के साथ "the" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q94. The more he earns, _______ more he spends.
questions.explanation: Structure "The + comparative... the + comparative" is used for proportional change. अनुपातिक बदलाव के लिए "The + comparative" का जोड़ा इस्तेमाल होता है।

questions.question: Q95. He is _______ honest man I have ever met.
questions.explanation: Superlative degree "most honest" is preceded by "the". Superlative degree "most honest" से पहले "the" का प्रयोग अनिवार्य है।

questions.question: Q96. Between RAM and SHYAM, who is _______?
questions.explanation: When comparing two specifically, use "the + comparative". दो व्यक्तियों के बीच निश्चित चुनाव के लिए "the + comparative" आता है।

questions.question: Q97. Of all the movies, this one is the _______ entertaining.
questions.explanation: For comparing more than two movies, use the superlative "most". दो से अधिक फिल्मों की तुलना में superlative "most" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q98. No other city in India is as _______ as Mumbai.
questions.explanation: Between "as...as", use the positive degree of the adjective. "As...as" के बीच हमेशा adjective की positive degree का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q99. Iron is _______ than any other metal.
questions.explanation: Use the comparative degree "more useful" when comparing with "any other". "Any other" के साथ तुलना करने पर comparative degree का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q100. This is the _______ of the two proposals.
questions.explanation: For two proposals, "the better" is the correct grammatical choice. दो प्रस्तावों के लिए "the better" व्याकरण के अनुसार सही विकल्प है।
